source_paper_title: Online Regret Bounds for Satisficing in Markov Decision Processes

Note that here our theoretical results may be suboptimal in the worst-case as we cannot achieve \(\widetilde O(\sqrt{T})\) worst-case expected regret. Further study in this direction is a valuable future work.

Further study to see the possibility of improving the regret to \(\widetilde O(\sqrt{T})\) is left as a future work.
title: Achieve square-root worst-case regret for optimistic bandit learning in AMDPs
background_latex:
Consider an episodic loop-free adversarial Markov decision process (AMDP) with a finite state space \(\mathcal X\) partitioned into \(L+1\) layers \(\mathcal X_0,\ldots,\mathcal X_L\), finite action space \(\mathcal A\), and a stationary transition kernel \(P(\cdot\mid x,a)\) that only moves from layer \(\ell\) to \(\ell+1\). Each episode \(t=1,\ldots,T\) has a (possibly adversarially chosen) cost function \(c_t:\mathcal X\times\mathcal A\to[0,1]\). A (stationary randomized) policy can be represented via its occupancy measure \(\rho\in\Delta(\mathcal M)\), where \(\rho(x,a)\) is the probability of visiting state-action pair \((x,a)\) in an episode under the policy and the transition kernel; \(\Delta(\mathcal M)\) denotes the set of feasible occupancy measures for the AMDP model.

In episode \(t\), the learner chooses an occupancy measure \(\rho_t\in\Delta(\mathcal M)\) (equivalently a policy \(\pi_t\)), interacts with the AMDP for one episode, and receives bandit feedback: it observes costs \(c_t(x,a)\) only on the realized trajectory \((x,a)\) pairs visited in that episode.

Regret against a fixed comparator occupancy measure \(\rho^*\in\Delta(\mathcal M)\) is
\[
R_T(\rho^*,\{c_t\}_{t=1}^T)=\sum_{t=1}^T \langle \rho_t-\rho^*,\, c_t\rangle,
\]
where \(\langle \rho,c\rangle = \sum_{x\in\mathcal X,\,a\in\mathcal A} \rho(x,a)c(x,a)\).

The learner is additionally given a sequence of cost predictors \(M_t:\mathcal X\times\mathcal A\to[0,1]\). Define the prediction error \(\sigma_t := c_t - M_t\). Optimistic regret bounds scale with some cumulative measure of \(\{\sigma_t\}\), yielding small regret when the predictors are accurate while still guaranteeing sublinear regret in the worst case.

In the paper’s bandit-feedback setting, the best proven worst-case exponents for optimistic regret are \(\widetilde O(T^{2/3})\) in expectation and \(\widetilde O(T^{3/4})\) with high probability, even when predictors are uninformative (so \(\|\sigma_t\|\) can be \(\Theta(1)\)). The authors explicitly ask whether these worst-case rates can be improved to the minimax \(\widetilde O(\sqrt T)\) rate while retaining an optimistic dependence on prediction error.
problem_definition_latex:
Design and analyze an online learning algorithm for loop-free episodic AMDPs with bandit feedback and access to cost predictors \(\{M_t\}_{t=1}^T\) that achieves an optimistic regret bound and, in the worst case over cost sequences \(\{c_t\}_{t=1}^T\), improves the proven \(\widetilde O(T^{2/3})\) expected regret and/or \(\widetilde O(T^{3/4})\) high-probability regret to \(\widetilde O(\sqrt T)\).

Formally, the goal is to obtain a bound on \(R_T(\rho^*,\{c_t\}_{t=1}^T)\) (in expectation and/or with probability at least \(1-\delta\)) whose leading-order worst-case dependence on \(T\) is \(\widetilde O(\sqrt T)\) when the predictors are uninformative (so \(\sigma_t\) is not small), while still scaling favorably with the cumulative prediction error when \(\sigma_t\) is small.
latex:
Consider an episodic loop-free adversarial Markov decision process (AMDP) with a finite state space \(\mathcal X\) partitioned into \(L+1\) layers \(\mathcal X_0,\ldots,\mathcal X_L\), finite action space \(\mathcal A\), and a stationary transition kernel \(P(\cdot\mid x,a)\) that only moves from layer \(\ell\) to \(\ell+1\). Design and analyze an online learning algorithm for loop-free episodic AMDPs with bandit feedback and access to cost predictors \(\{M_t\}_{t=1}^T\) that achieves an optimistic regret bound and, in the worst case over cost sequences \(\{c_t\}_{t=1}^T\), improves the proven \(\widetilde O(T^{2/3})\) expected regret and/or \(\widetilde O(T^{3/4})\) high-probability regret to \(\widetilde O(\sqrt T)\).

Formally, the goal is to obtain a bound on \(R_T(\rho^*,\{c_t\}_{t=1}^T)\) (in expectation and/or with probability at least \(1-\delta\)) whose leading-order worst-case dependence on \(T\) is \(\widetilde O(\sqrt T)\) when the predictors are uninformative (so \(\sigma_t\) is not small), while still scaling favorably with the cumulative prediction error when \(\sigma_t\) is small.
location_in_paper: Page 7, Section 4 (discussion after Theorem 2); and page 8, Section 4 (discussion after Theorem 3).
open_problem_quote:
Note that here our theoretical results may be suboptimal in the worst-case as we cannot achieve \(\widetilde O(\sqrt{T})\) worst-case expected regret. Further study in this direction is a valuable future work.

Further study to see the possibility of improving the regret to \(\widetilde O(\sqrt{T})\) is left as a future work.
subject_classification: Online learning in adversarial Markov decision processes (reinforcement learning)
counterexample_suitability_rationale: This is an algorithm-design and upper-bound question rather than a crisp universal inequality stated for a fixed class; there is no finite instance class where one can efficiently falsify a candidate theorem by brute-force search. Lower-bound counterexamples may exist, but automated search would require modeling an adaptive adversary and optimizing over policies, which is not straightforward to scale.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate algorithms can be implemented and empirically evaluated on simulated AMDP instances with predictors, giving an automatic fitness signal (empirical regret). However, the target is a provable \(\widetilde O(\sqrt T)\) worst-case (and optimistic) guarantee, and automatically verifying such a theorem is not feasible; evolutionary search could help generate ideas but not certify the bound.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
